question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findStorage(EntityId, byte[])] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findStorage() does not validate that the key byte[] is exactly 32 bytes (EVM slot size), allowing them to supply a 1-byte key that matches a DB record with a 1-byte slot column, potentially reading unintended storage entries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findStorage(EntityId, byte[])] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the contractSlotsCache stores per-contract caches as values (CaffeineCache objects), and by querying 3000 unique contractIds, fill the contractSlotsCache to its maximum size, causing subsequent contract queries to evict existing entries and lose their slot key sets, forcing full DB queries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findStorage(EntityId, byte[])] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findStorageBatch() does not deduplicate the cachedSlots list before passing it to findStorageBatch(), so if the same ByteBuffer appears multiple times in the keySet (due to a cache implementation bug), the SQL IN-clause contains duplicate parameters, potentially causing unexpected query behavior?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findStorage(EntityId, byte[])] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findStorage() with batch caching enabled does not check if the contractStateCache entry is EMPTY_VALUE before calling findStorageBatch(), so a slot key that was previously found to have no value (stored as EMPTY_VALUE) still triggers a batch DB query on every request after the 2s cache expiry?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findStorage(EntityId, byte[])] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findStorageBatch() uses contractSlotsCache.getNativeCache().asMap().keySet() to get all cached slot keys, and this is a live view of the cache's internal map, so concurrent modifications during iteration may cause keys to be skipped or duplicated in the cachedSlots list?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findStorage(EntityId, byte[])] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findStorage() returns Optional<byte[]> where the byte[] is a direct reference to the cached array, allowing the caller to mutate the array in-place and corrupt the cache entry, affecting all subsequent callers who read the same cached value?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findStorage(EntityId, byte[])] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findStorageBatch() creates a new ArrayList for cachedSlots on every call, and if the slotsPerContract cache contains 1500 entries, this allocates a 1500-element ArrayList on every cache miss, causing excessive GC pressure under high load?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findStorage(EntityId, byte[])] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findStorage() does not enforce any per-user or per-IP rate limit, allowing them to issue thousands of requests per second to enumerate contract storage slots, causing sustained high DB load?
